answer: The correct answer is \boxed{J}. Municipal bond interest is generally exempt from federal income tax, so for a corporation in the 50% tax bracket, a 4% municipal bond has a tax-equivalent yield of 8%, which is higher than the after-tax return of any 5% taxable option. All taxable 5% options yield only 2.5% after the 50% corporate tax rate. Therefore, the 4% municipal bond provides the greatest net return.
